rejected_candidates.title: Unverified setSecurity allows arbitrary contract assignment
rejected_candidates.reason: `setSecurity()` is an `onlyMaster` governance action; this is a trust-model issue, not a permissionless protocol vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Liquidation state inconsistency - token transfer after state update
rejected_candidates.reason: If `_safeTransferFrom()` fails, the EVM reverts the whole call and all earlier state changes are rolled back, so no persistent inconsistency is created.

rejected_candidates.title: Division by zero in fee share calculation
rejected_candidates.reason: With non-malicious fees at or below 100%, the denominator stays positive because `pseudoTotalPool` is increased before the division; reaching zero requires privileged fee misconfiguration.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing reentrancy protection in receive function
rejected_candidates.reason: `receive()` mutates no lending state and only forwards ETH with `transfer`; forced ETH acceptance is not an exploitable protocol bug here.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing zero address validation in approve function
rejected_candidates.reason: Approving the zero address is at most a harmless user mistake and does not create protocol-level harm.

rejected_candidates.title: No emergency stop mechanism
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a best-practice/governance observation rather than a concrete vulnerability in the current code.

rejected_candidates.title: Liquidation transaction can be delayed indefinitely
rejected_candidates.reason: Mempool delay is not controlled by contract code and no concrete exploit path is created by the implementation.

rejected_candidates.title: Collateral factor allows 100%
rejected_candidates.reason: The collateral factor is a governance-set risk parameter; allowing exactly 100% is a configuration choice, not a permissionless exploit.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing event emissions for critical functions
rejected_candidates.reason: Missing events affect observability only and do not directly endanger funds or protocol solvency.

rejected_candidates.title: Renounce ownership is permanent with no recovery
rejected_candidates.reason: Permanent ownership renunciation is expected behavior and a governance/trust-model decision, not a code vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Fee manager NFT ID assumption
rejected_candidates.reason: This code intentionally hardcodes fee accounting to NFT id 0, and no evidence in the provided code shows that assumption is invalid.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential storage collision with isolated pools
rejected_candidates.reason: `positionLocked` is intentionally reused as the isolation-pool registration/lock bit; no unintended conflicting storage use was found.
